judgment.summary: Profitable company with strong free cash flow and a low valuation, but facing declining earnings growth.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates; next earnings date is 2026-07-28.
positives: Trailing P/E 8.33x and Forward P/E 7.69x suggest a very low valuation.
PEG ratio of 0.81 indicates the stock may be undervalued relative to its earnings growth.
Free cash flow of $4.08B TTM is substantial.
Return on equity at 25.1% is strong.
Dividend yield of 1.3% offers some return to shareholders.
negatives: Revenue growth is only 7.2% YoY, with recent quarterly earnings growth at -13.5%.
Analyst growth estimates for the current quarter and fiscal year are negative.
Quick ratio of 0.22 suggests potential short-term liquidity issues.

news.paragraph: Michael Burry's Scion Asset Management has taken a new stake in PayPal, according to a recent filing. This news arrives as the company is reportedly preparing for an IPO, though the headlines focus on SpaceX's potential offering. Traders should monitor PayPal's upcoming earnings report for any signs of a turnaround in its growth trajectory.

headlines.title: Factbox-IPO-bound SpaceX's board is stacked with Musk empire loyalists
SpaceX IPO Set to Deliver Massive Returns to Investors
PayPal Holdings Inc. (PYPL): Michael Burry Initiates New Position

examples.body: $BABA $PYPL this kind of thing is the problem with not valuing companies correctly . Essentially risk free Baba went to $190, Same thing here.
$PYPL is about to explode higher so be ready
$INTU now you guys know how $PYPL and $ADBE investors feel. Fundamentals get better but the stock sells off. Oh and the P/E and P/FCF ratios along with buyback…
$PYPL 525k shares to be purchased today and another 500k tomorrow.
